data.title: Epigenetics, Vitamin C, and Abnormal Hematopoiesis - Role of Vitamin C in Epigenetic Regulation in Hematopoiesis Sub-Study on CCUS, Low-Risk MDS, and CMML-0/1
data.brief_summary: The primary purpose of this multi-centre, randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind phase II study is to investigate if oral vitamin C may change the biology of low-risk myeloid malignancies; i.e., clonal cytopenia of undetermined significance (CCUS), low-risk myelodysplastic syndromes (MDS), and chronic myelomonocytic leukemia (CMML)-0/1 by reversing the epigenetic changes characteristic of these disease entities. The epigenetic regulator TET2 is the gene most often affected in CCUS. Preclin
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

A diagnosis of CCUS:

* Persistent cytopenia for \> 6 months defined as hgb \< 11.3 g/dL (7 mmol/L) in women and hgb \< 12.9 g/dL (8 mmol/L) in men, thrombocyte count \< 150 x 10\^9/L or neutrophil count \< 1.8 x 10\^9/L
* Normal cytogenetics (with the exception of deletion of t
endpoints: Median Change from Baseline in Variant Allele Frequency at 12 Months

data.title: Increasing Animal Source Foods in Diets of HIV-Infected Kenyan Women and Their Children
data.brief_summary: Many of the 28 million people with immunodeficiency virus (HIV) and acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) estimated to be living in sub-Saharan Africa also suffer from malnutrition. Reproductive age women, their infants and young children are among the most vulnerable for malnutrition and progression of HIV to AIDS and mortality is increased in the malnourished, as seen in Eastern and Southern Africa. The HIV Nutrition Project (HNP) research evaluates the effect of protein and micronutrients
data.eligibility_criteria:
HIV-infected women and their children who live in the Turbo catchment area in the Uasin Gishu District of Kenya and attend the AMPATH clinic there will be invited to participate in the study.

Inclusion Criteria:

* Women will be included if drug naïve and classified as WHO Stage 1 or 2 54 with a CD
endpoints: The impact of meat intake on mid-upper-arm muscle area; comparisons between groups, of changes from baseline to 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18 mos (intervention); 24 mos (6 mos post intervention).

data.title: Neurodevelopmental Outcome of Early Dietary Lysine Restriction in Pyridoxine
data.country: Switzerland, United Kingdom, Canada, Netherlands, Germany, United States
data.brief_summary: Restricting dietary lysine intake in infants from age 3 months or less with confirmed diagnosis of pyridoxine-dependent epilepsy due to Antiquitin (ATQ) deficiency will: reduce the accumulation of neurotoxic substratesα-aminoadipicsemialdehydeandits cyclic equivalent 1-piperideine-6-carboxylate;and will improve overall neurodevelopmental outcome at 3 years of age by acting as an effective intervention into the complex pathophysiology of the condition.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Diagnosis of pyridoxine-dependent epilepsy based on clinical symptoms and elevated levels of plasma or urine AASA. Confirmation by at least one known disease causing mutation in the ALDH7A1 gene to be obtained within one month of enrollment.
* Participant is male or female \<3
endpoints: Level of biochemical marker-α- aminoadipicsemialdehyde (AASA) in plasma and urine
